items.why_it_matters: Beauty buyers who name the actual concern are usually easier to route into a useful next step than users who only say they want to look better.
items.echoer_action: Acknowledge the concern and keep gathering time and branch facts.

items.why_it_matters: Appointment timing is one of the clearest signs that a salon inquiry is moving toward action.
items.echoer_action: Keep the reply warm and direct the next step toward booking facts.

items.why_it_matters: First-time caution means the reply should optimize for reassurance and trust, not speed or upsell pressure.
items.echoer_action: Use calm language and guide toward a consult-style next step.

items.why_it_matters: Event-driven salon requests often convert quickly if the time window is handled well.
items.echoer_action: Acknowledge urgency and ask for date plus available slot.

items.why_it_matters: Price-only questions are not low intent by default, but they need concern scope before the next step becomes useful.
items.echoer_action: Answer lightly and recover one scope detail.

items.why_it_matters: When the concern is personal and exposed in public, the response must protect dignity while staying useful.
items.echoer_action: Reply gently, avoid repeating the wording harshly, and move toward a safer follow-up path.
